title: Postcards From The Wedge

jokes.text: D'oh.
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific

jokes.text: These two men might as well be kissing.
jokes.punchline: Absurd conclusion about men standing close together
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about crowded public transport with absurd escalation

jokes.text: Looks like that robot arm needs a little adjustment.
jokes.setup: Futuristic car picking up child from school
jokes.punchline: Robot arm apparently malfunctions in some comedic way
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons visual gag with deadpan narration acknowledging the failure

jokes.text: Well, that concludes... I don't really know what that was.
jokes.punchline: Teacher admits confusion about what they just watched
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor acknowledging the absurdity of the opening segment

jokes.text: Mine features indigenous Hopi songs. * And a hie na ho na hey. *
jokes.setup: Martin showing off his elaborate school project
jokes.punchline: Fake Native American song sounds ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Martin's over-achievement and cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: a brilliant little boy receives an 'A' for his amazing presentation.
jokes.punchline: Calling himself 'brilliant' in third person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin characterization - his narcissistic self-praise disguised as educational presentation

jokes.text: I can build one of those things from the crap in my desk.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes he forgot his project
jokes.punchline: Overconfident assessment using 'crap'
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - his delusional optimism and casual profanity

jokes.text: Bart Simpson, you've had three months to do this project. You started 30 seconds ago.
jokes.punchline: Precise timing of his last-minute effort
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and timing, perfectly captures student procrastination

jokes.text: Thank you. Thank you. I'm not complimenting you.
jokes.setup: Bart thanks teacher for her observation
jokes.punchline: Teacher clarifies she wasn't being positive
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke with perfect economy and character dynamics

jokes.text: Ha-ha. You're old. I saw it on video in the '80s. Outdated medium. I stand by my ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Teacher tries to defend against age insult
jokes.punchline: Bart doubles down by calling video outdated too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and commitment to the bit, very Bart-like stubbornness

jokes.text: Hah. I wasn't born yesterday. Hah. Tell me about it.
jokes.setup: Teacher uses cliché about not being naive
jokes.punchline: Bart agrees sarcastically about her age
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the cliché, continuing the age theme

jokes.text: Sixth-graders? No!!! I'm sure it's a training bra. PG-13. I have an unwanted boner.
jokes.setup: Bart sees sixth grade girls during fire alarm
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward stream of consciousness about puberty
jokes.explanation: Bold cringe humor about pre-teen sexuality, escalating awkwardness

jokes.text: Ay, there's no better feeling on earth.
jokes.punchline: Extreme enthusiasm for mundane job task
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing working class pride taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Whatever happened to 'Hi'? Hi. Now what are you up to?
jokes.punchline: Immediately does what he suggested then repeats question
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure and character consistency - Lisa's politeness vs. practicality

jokes.text: Whatever happened to 'Hi'? Hi. Now what are you up to?
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives perfunctory greeting then asks same question
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about social niceties that perfectly fits Milhouse's character

jokes.text: Just close your eyes and think of Milhouse.
jokes.punchline: Twisted version of 'think of England' sexual advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic phrase with perfect inappropriate context

jokes.text: How did you...? I don't want to talk about it.
jokes.punchline: Bart's horrified realization of the method
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke, Bart's dawning horror

jokes.text: I tried really hard to stop you from finding out. Does that help?
jokes.punchline: Bart thinks effort to hide it should count for something
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - thinking deception effort deserves credit

jokes.text: synonyms, mean-the-sames, define-a-likes, word twins
jokes.punchline: Making up increasingly silly names for synonyms
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating wordplay showing Homer's limited vocabulary creatively

jokes.text: I want him to be Korean by the time he's done.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to pile on more homework
jokes.punchline: Racist stereotype about Korean academic achievement
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotype but delivered with Homer's characteristic ignorance

jokes.text: Oh, my. A child who doesn't enjoy school? Hello? Hollywood? You want to buy the movie rights to this incredible story? A million dollars? Deal. Oh, now I have to write it.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests Bart might not like school
jokes.punchline: Homer imagines this mundane situation as movie-worthy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer's delusional get-rich-quick thinking

jokes.text: Wow, how does this show stay so fresh?
jokes.punchline: Praising obviously repetitive/bad content
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that could apply to The Simpsons itself, plus Homer's low standards

jokes.text: Until your homework is done, the only place your butt is safe from my foot is in that chair.
jokes.punchline: Creatively threatening specific violence
jokes.explanation: Good parenting threat with creative phrasing, very Homer-like

jokes.text: Hey, Lis, Mom and Dad just told me two different things. How is that possible?
jokes.setup: Bart confused by conflicting parental messages
jokes.punchline: Treating parental disagreement as impossible phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's central conflict, Bart's naive perspective

jokes.text: Well, sometimes people who agree on most things have one issue that they disagree strongly about. In politics, it's called a wedge issue, like illegal immigration or gay marriage.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated political analysis for family dispute
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - applying academic political theory to family dynamics

jokes.text: In politics, it's called a wedge issue, like illegal immigration or gay marriage.
jokes.punchline: Episode title reference and real political examples
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-reference to episode's theme delivered through Lisa's characteristic intelligence

jokes.text: Can I play them against each other so I don't have to do any homework at all?
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately sees manipulative opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - immediately seeing selfish angle in political concept

jokes.text: Hey, hey, I would end all life on this planet just to get out of doing fractions.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions Bart's willingness to hurt his parents' marriage
jokes.punchline: Extreme escalation about avoiding math
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Bart's priorities, perfectly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: End... all... life... on... this... planet!
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to explain fractions aren't hard
jokes.punchline: Bart doubles down on his apocalyptic stance
jokes.explanation: Great callback with emphasis showing Bart's commitment to the bit

jokes.text: You'll need to know fractions to make that explosion!
jokes.setup: Bart wants to end all life
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out he'd need math for mass destruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa comeback - logical and ironic, undermining Bart's plan with education

jokes.text: Too late, Marge. I already used a prayer block on your prayer.
jokes.setup: Marge praying for Homer to see she's right
jokes.punchline: Homer treats prayer like a strategic game
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist concept treating divine intervention like a video game mechanic

jokes.text: Smooth move, Mom, tricking Dad into having lunch at the one place that doesn't serve beer.
jokes.punchline: Bart recognizes Marge's passive-aggressive tactic
jokes.explanation: Good observation about marital warfare, Bart understanding adult dynamics

jokes.text: By express, I mean that you express your anger and hatred.
jokes.punchline: Explanation reveals it's about emotional expression, not speed
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay subversion fitting Moe's depressing establishment

jokes.text: Well, how could I ever hit the hamper if you don't let me practice?
jokes.setup: Marge complains about Homer's aim with laundry
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic about needing practice to throw underwear
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic applied to mundane domestic issue

jokes.text: Congratulations. You're officially a sociopath. Hey, at least I'm on a path.
jokes.punchline: Homer finds positive spin on the insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay with perfect Homer optimism in the face of serious accusation

jokes.text: You can't sex fire me, I sex quit.
jokes.punchline: Homer treats it like employment using 'sex' as prefix
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay treating intimate relationship like business transaction

jokes.text: You know, maybe I should use a towel marked 'BDD': big diddily deal.
jokes.punchline: Creates acronym using his verbal tic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ned characterization incorporating his 'diddily' speech pattern into wordplay

jokes.text: Let me say to you all, I'm pretty sure this is not a dream.
jokes.punchline: Addressing audience about dream status while asleep
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about dream sequences, though somewhat confused execution

jokes.text: Compromise has no place in a marriage. Stick to your guns.
jokes.punchline: Terrible advice from perpetually single sisters
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic advice from characters who are terrible at relationships

jokes.text: Once again, sleeping at work has saved my marriage.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he should apologize to Marge
jokes.punchline: Credits his laziness with relationship insight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding positive spin on his worst qualities

jokes.text: That pacifier's the last true friend you'll have.
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives baby nihilistic life advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - cynical wisdom delivered to innocent baby

jokes.text: He's the reason we had to get married.
jokes.setup: Homer saying Bart drove them apart
jokes.punchline: Reminder that Bart caused the marriage via teen pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to show's backstory, ironic reversal of the situation

jokes.text: Bart? Bart who? I'm being cute. It's working.
jokes.setup: Homer pretends not to know who Bart is
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges and evaluates his own attempt at humor
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Homer commenting on his own comedic performance

jokes.text: From now on, you're on your own. Really? Okay.
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate acceptance with no protest
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing both characters' dysfunction - abandonment and acceptance

jokes.text: Their tiny tongues are like knives.
jokes.punchline: Absurd description of hummingbird anatomy as weaponry
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation turning cute birds into terrifying attackers

jokes.text: I once waited an hour and a half for a haircut.
jokes.setup: Skinner claiming to be very patient
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane example of 'extreme' patience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - treating ordinary experience as remarkable achievement

jokes.text: With no Sir Topham Hatt to tell us what we can and can't do.
jokes.setup: Describing underground like Thomas the Tank Engine
jokes.punchline: Reference to controlling authority figure from children's show
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's childish perspective and rebellion fantasy

jokes.text: Which flavor do I save: the Radical Red or the Blueberry Blast? Oh, curse this Squishee's choice.
jokes.punchline: Treats trivial choice as philosophical dilemma, pun on Sophie's Choice
jokes.explanation: Clever pun on Sophie's Choice applied to mundane beverage decision

jokes.text: Oh, curse this Squishee's choice.
jokes.setup: Bart can't decide which Squishee flavor to save
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Sophie's choice' with convenience store drink
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference applied to trivial kid dilemma - great wordplay

jokes.text: It reminds me of watching pictures download on our old dial-up modem. Remember that day we brought it home, and it was all... eeee... ooo... eee... ooo.
jokes.setup: Homer comparing Marge's knitting to something
jokes.punchline: Specific memory of dial-up modem sounds
jokes.explanation: Decent nostalgia humor about outdated technology, though somewhat dated reference

jokes.text: Don't know, don't care.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to tell Homer about his adventures
jokes.punchline: Complete parental disinterest despite unusual circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's commitment to ignoring Bart

jokes.text: Bart, at the end of the day, if your parents aren't p.o.'d, you haven't really pranked.
jokes.setup: Bart feeling unsatisfied with his pranks
jokes.punchline: Milhouse offers philosophical insight about pranking
jokes.explanation: Good wisdom from unexpected source, Milhouse understanding prank psychology

jokes.text: Destroy the school, eh?
jokes.setup: Learning Bart plans to destroy the school
jokes.punchline: Skinner's intrigued rather than horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - principal being curious rather than protective of school

jokes.text: We should both take a role in vacation planning.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to work on marriage while saving Bart
jokes.punchline: Offering completely mundane marital improvement while in crisis
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - offering token gesture during actual emergency

jokes.text: Oh, there's a nickel.
jokes.setup: Dramatic rescue mission in dangerous underground
jokes.punchline: Homer gets distracted by small change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - materialism trumping parental responsibility even in crisis

jokes.text: Ah, you guys are the meanest parents ever. Thank you.
jokes.punchline: Homer takes the insult as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's twisted pride in poor parenting

jokes.text: Leave it to you to misspell a word you see first thing every morning.
jokes.punchline: Points out he misspelled 'principal' despite seeing Skinner daily
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Bart's spelling incompetence with logical setup

tmdb_overview: When Bart fails to turn in his homework, Mrs. Krabappel sends a letter home about Bart's behaviour. Despite Bart's best efforts to intercept it, Homer reads the letter, and he and Marge visit Principal Skinner for a parent-teacher conference. Furious, Homer punishes him, but Marge takes a more sympathetic approach. When Bart realizes he can pit Homer and Marge against each other to his benefit, his scheming reaches new heights.
